title: Hybrid Search Setup
concept: Running dense similarity query and sparse BM25 query in parallel and merging them using RRF.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of a hybrid search system?

options.text: To merge different search techniques for better results
To only use one search technique at a time

exercises.explain: A hybrid search system combines techniques to improve the quality of search results.

cards.title: What is Hybrid Search?
cards.body: Hybrid search uses multiple techniques to find information effectively. This means combining methods to take advantage of their strengths.

cards.title: Dense and Sparse Queries
cards.body: Dense queries look to find similar items in a continuous space using embeddings, while sparse queries, like BM25, compare exact or near-exact matches using term frequency.

cards.title: The Supermarket Analogy
cards.body: Imagine a supermarket that uses both aisle signs (sparse queries) to find specific items and a map (dense queries) to understand the layout for better overall searching.

cards.title: Example of Hybrid Search
cards.body: In a PDF search, a dense query could find similar sentences, while BM25 could retrieve important keywords. Together, they provide a broader search result.

cards.title: Myth: One Search Method is Enough
cards.body: Relying on just one search method can limit results. Hybrid searching helps in finding better answers by combining techniques.

cards.title: Visualizing Hybrid Search
cards.body: Hybrid search combines sparse and dense queries, optimizing search results.

left.text: Looks for similar sentences in context.

right.text: Looks for exact matches using keywords.

exercises.prompt: What does RRF stand for in hybrid search?
exercises.explain: RRF stands for Ranked Retrieval Function, which is used to merge results from different search methods.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A hybrid search setup can improve the effectiveness of information retrieval.
exercises.explain: True, a hybrid search combines strengths from both dense and sparse techniques for better outcomes.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Hybrid search uses both ____ and sparse queries to improve search results.
exercises.sentence: Hybrid search uses both dense and sparse queries to improve search results.
exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'dense' because hybrid searches use both types of queries.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following statements about hybrid search is AI-generated?

options.text: Hybrid search only uses sparse queries for better results.
Combining dense and sparse queries makes search results more accurate.
Dense queries are always slower than sparse queries.

exercises.explain: The AI-generated option accurately highlights the benefit of combining different types of queries for improved accuracy.

exercises.prompt: In a hybrid search setup, which approach allows you to merge results effectively to enhance retrieval accuracy?

options.text: Using RRF to combine results from both dense and sparse queries
Only focusing on dense queries for all responses
Limiting the search to just one technique at a time

exercises.explain: Using RRF to combine results from both dense and sparse queries allows you to enhance retrieval accuracy by leveraging the strengths of both methods.
